pattern: The count of structurally distinct binary search trees on n keys is the nth Catalan number, summed over every choice of root.
hints: Pick which key sits at the root; everything smaller forms its left subtree and everything larger its right.
The number of trees for a given size depends only on the size, so let f(k) be the count for k nodes and combine left and right counts.
f(0)=f(1)=1 and f(n) = sum over i of f(i-1)*f(n-i); this recurrence is the Catalan number sequence.

edge_inputs.description: growth is Catalan, not factorial or power of two

stress.note: Catalan(19) fits comfortably in a 64-bit and js double integer range
